L1.area: Content management object persistence and form data processing layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted directory traversal sequences in user-supplied file name parameters. Specially crafted input containing sequences to escape the intended directory can interact with server-side image processing scripts. The attack can be facilitated when the server's PHP installation supports upload progress tracking.

L3.area: IPF (ImpressCMS Persistence Framework) controller and object management layer
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted directory traversal sequences in user-submitted file name fields during form data processing for image and file data types. The traversal sequences escape the intended upload directory to reach server-side image processing scripts, and the attack payload may leverage PHP session upload progress functionality.

ground_truth.reason: The postDataToObject method in the IPF Controller does not sanitize user-supplied file name parameters for directory traversal sequences (e.g., .....///), allowing attackers to escape the intended upload directory and interact with arbitrary server-side scripts.
